messages.content: Add an FAQ section with 4 common questions people ask before starting a vending machine business.

context.title: Coffee vending for offices
context.metaDescription: Coffee vending machines for modern workplaces.

props.heading: Old headline
props.body: Coffee vending machines for offices keep teams stocked with fresh drinks without anyone owning a brewing rota. A managed machine covers restocking, cleaning, and fault response so facilities can stay focused on the building.

input.title: Frequently asked questions about office coffee vending
input.body: Common questions about managing workplace refreshment solutions.

faqItems.question: What are the space and utility requirements for installing machines?
faqItems.answer: The space required depends on the machine size and access needs. Typically, you need a level floor area with access to a standard electrical outlet and, for plumbed units, a nearby water connection. It is important to measure your intended site's clearance for door swings and service access during the initial site assessment.

Most professional operators offer tiered service agreements. These typically include scheduled replenishments of coffee beans, milk, and cups, along with periodic deep cleaning and preventative maintenance visits. Having a clear service schedule helps ensure consistent product availability while minimizing machine downtime.
faqItems.question: How do restock and maintenance schedules work?
What payment options are available for employees?
faqItems.answer: Many modern machines offer flexible payment options to accommodate different workplace policies. You can configure machines for cashless payments, employee-subsidized pricing, or even free-vend setups where the company covers the cost. Choosing the right payment model depends on whether you want to encourage office culture or partially offset operational costs.

Choosing the right equipment depends on your daily drink volume and the variety of beverages you want to offer. High-traffic offices may require larger bean-to-cup systems, while smaller teams might prefer compact units that focus on specific drink types. During a site consultation, professionals can assess your usage patterns to recommend the most reliable hardware.
faqItems.question: How do I choose the right coffee machine for my office size?
